steps.title: Identify the opening em-dash
steps.content: An opening em-dash appears before “first published”: …—first published in [BLANK] is essential... The opening punctuation signals a parenthetical aside requiring a matching close.

steps.title: Apply the em-dash pairing rule
steps.content: A parenthetical aside opened with an em-dash must be closed with a matching em-dash. Choice A (1689—) correctly closes the structure: …—first published in 1689—is essential reading... Choices B, C, and D fail to complete the parenthetical by omitting the em-dash or using incompatible punctuation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
